name: Will County

actions.title: (Village of Elwood) Data center moratorium
actions.summary: The Village of Elwood enacted a moratorium on new data center developments to evaluate the strain on local infrastructure, particularly water and electricity, before approving additional projects.

contextBlurb: Will County, south of Chicago, has seen significant data center interest in communities like Elwood. Local officials have raised concerns about water and power demands from large-scale facilities on formerly agricultural land.

name: Grundy County

actions.title: (City of Morris) Microsoft data center campus approval
actions.summary: The City of Morris approved zoning and site plans for a major Microsoft data center campus, with the company investing over $1 billion in the development on land near existing power infrastructure.

contextBlurb: Grundy County has attracted hyperscale data center investment due to proximity to nuclear power generation and available land. Microsoft's investment near Morris represents one of the largest data center commitments in central Illinois.

name: DeKalb County

actions.title: (City of DeKalb) Meta data center tax incentive agreement
actions.summary: The City of DeKalb approved tax incentive agreements for Meta's large-scale data center campus, which has undergone multiple phases of expansion in the area.

contextBlurb: DeKalb County hosts Meta's massive data center campus, one of the largest in Illinois. The facility has drawn attention for its water consumption and energy demands, alongside the economic benefits it brings to the community.

name: Kendall County

actions.title: (Village of Minooka) Data center zoning review
actions.summary: Officials in the Minooka area have been reviewing zoning regulations to accommodate proposed data center developments while addressing resident concerns about noise, water use, and land conversion from agricultural use.

contextBlurb: Kendall County, part of the fast-growing Chicago exurban corridor, has seen data center proposals that test existing agricultural zoning frameworks. Residents have voiced concerns about the industrial character of data centers in rural settings.
